intro: Cirsium souliei, also known as the Chinese thistle or Sichuan thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. Bioactive constituents of Cirsium souliei include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, or used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and infusions, which are used to promote digestion, relieve pain, and boost the immune system.
